etymology.description: semantic 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass") + phonetic 羅(羅) (luó) -> vines or creeping plants
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass") indicates the plant or botanical aspect, while the phonetic component 羅(羅) (luó) provides the pronunciation. This combination suggests a connection to creeping plants such as vines, emphasizing their vegetation nature.
etymology.simplification: Simplified 蘿(羅) (luó, "vines") to 萝 by retaining 艹 while simplifying 羅 to 罗 for reduced complexity.

examples.chinese: 这道菜需要一些白萝卜(這道菜需要一些白蘿蔔)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dào cài xūyào yīxiē bái luóbo.
examples.english: This dish needs some white radishes.

examples.chinese: 花园里长满了藤萝(花園裡長滿了藤蘿)，它们的气味很香(它們的氣味很香)。
examples.pinyin: Huāyuán lǐ zhǎng mǎn le téng luó, tāmen de qìwèi hěn xiāng.
examples.english: The garden is full of vines, and they smell very fragrant.

examples.chinese: 炒萝卜丝是一道典型的家常菜(炒蘿蔔絲是一道典型的家常菜)。
examples.pinyin: Chǎo luóbo sī shì yī dào diǎnxíng de jiācháng cài.
examples.english: Stir-fried radish strips is a typical home-cooked dish.
